'''Dean Lawford''' (born 9 May 1977) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level for [[Dewsbury Moor A.R.L.F.C.|Dewsbury Moor]] of the [[National Conference League]], [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Bramley RLFC|Bramley]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Batley Bulldogs]], [[Widnes Vikings]] and [[Dewsbury Rams]], as a {{rlp|sh}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Lawford was born in [[Dewsbury]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Career==
Lawford was transferred from [[Dewsbury Moor A.R.L.F.C.|Dewsbury Moor]] to [[Sheffield Eagles]] in 1994, he was transferred from Sheffield Eagles to [[Leeds Rhinos]] in December 1996.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Hetherington signs three players from Eagles |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-hetherington-signs-three-players-from-eagles-1314224.html |website=The Independent |access-date=16 May 2023 |date=12 December 1996}}</ref>

In August 2002, he signed a two-year contract with [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Perkins |first1=Eric |title=Widnes finally land Lawford |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/widnes-finally-land-lawford-173972.html |website=The Independent |access-date=16 May 2023 |date=20 August 2002}}</ref> He was released by the club at his own request in March 2004.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Woods |first1=Dave |title=Lawford walks out on Widnes |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/widnes/3501890.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=16 May 2023 |date=11 March 2004}}</ref>
